hints: A while loop needs three parts: set up n before the loop, write a condition (n >= 1), and change n inside the loop body (n -= 1).
Print n before decrementing it. If you write n -= 1 first, you'll print the wrong values.
After the loop body runs, Python re-checks the condition. When n reaches 0, the condition n >= 1 is false and the loop stops.
If your function prints nothing, the while condition might be false from the start. Check that n starts at a positive value and your condition is n >= 1.
